prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='CWT_rf', seasonality_champion='ONLY_DWT_svr', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.72) and high seasonality variance (0.89), with ARIMA dominating overall RMSE while CWT_rf leads trend tracking and ONLY_DWT_svr excels at seasonality capture. The early/late horizon specialists differ (ARIMA vs CWT_rf), supporting DBA combination which can leverage model diversity. Tier1 models should receive higher weights, but the high disagreement metrics indicate that simple averaging would be suboptimal.

  "tier2_models": ["catboost", "rf", "DWT_rf", "CWT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_svr", "NaiveSeasonal", "svr", "CWT_svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.7222,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["down", "up", "up"]

  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.72) and high seasonality variance (0.89), with ARIMA dominating overall RMSE while CWT_rf leads trend tracking and ONLY_DWT_svr excels at seasonality capture. The early/late horizon specialists differ (ARIMA vs CWT_rf), supporting DBA combination which can leverage model diversity. Tier1 models should receive higher weights, but the high disagreement metrics indicate that simple averaging would be suboptimal."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3"],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=10 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity, supporting per-horizon selection methods. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high seasonality variance (0.89) and model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.72). With n_windows=3, small top_k=3 provides stability. RMSE spread between ARIMA(680.37) and ONLY_FT_catboost(1135.42) is 67%, justifying rmse_focus preset. DBA handles phase-shifted predictions while inverse_rmse_weights adds risk-adjusted weighting."
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.72) and high seasonality variance (0.89), with ARIMA dominating overall RMSE while CWT_rf leads trend tracking and ONLY_DWT_svr excels at seasonality capture. The early/late horizon specialists differ (ARIMA vs CWT_rf), supporting DBA combination which can leverage model diversity. Tier1 models should receive higher weights, but the high disagreement metrics indicate that simple averaging would be suboptimal.
